reference: Hi, I did a pregnancy test which turned out positive. I further did an ultrasound, and my report stated the following: pelvic ultrasound shows an anteverted uterus with a thickened endometrium. The left adnexal region showed a thick-walled cystic lesion with a ring of fire around an embryo and evidence of significant fluid in the cul-de-sac. Number of embryos: single. Location: left adnexal region. Gestational sac: eight point four one mm. Gestational age: four weeks. Comments: single left ectopic gestation at four weeks. What does this mean? I am really worried about my pregnancy being ectopic. What should I do?
text: Hi, I recently had an ultrasound and my report stated the following: Pelvic ultrasound shows an anteverted uterus with thickened endometrium. The left adnexal region showed a thick-walled cystic lesion with a ring of fire around embryo and evidence of significant fluid in culdesac. Crown-rump embryo: Single. Location: Left adnexal region. Gestational sac: 8.4 mm. Gestational age: 4 weeks. Comments: Single left ectopic gestation, 4 weeks. I am really worried about my pregnancy being ectopic. What should I do?
